title: marketing — TenancyHook + lifecycle hooks
description: Cross-module hook implementations. TenancyHooks are the tenancy module's contract for reacting to tenant-lifecycle events (provision / suspend / erase). Marketing registers three hooks: tenant erasure cascade, consent-revocation suppression, and tenant-suspend provider deactivation. No bootstrapper — marketing does not need per-request tenant hydration.

hooks.name: PurgeMarketingDataForErasedTenant
hooks.purpose: Cascade delete via FK CASCADE across the four marketing tables. This hook writes the compliance audit trail entry + emits count metrics per entity family (marketing_events / marketing_deliveries / marketing_provider_configs / marketing_dead_letters purged) + fires best-effort provider-side deletion requests (Meta Data Deletion API, Google Ads user-data deletion, TikTok user-data deletion) so the propagation is transparent to auditors.
hooks.compliance: GDPR Art. 17 (right to erasure). Emitted metrics feed the compliance dashboard for retention proof. Provider-side deletion is best-effort — Stackra records the deletion request; the provider is responsible for honoring it. If a provider does not respond within its documented SLA, ops files a support ticket.

hooks.name: SuppressMarketingOnConsentRevoked
hooks.purpose: Compliance-critical. Reacts to consent revocation on the `marketing` or `advertising` category. Sweeps pending + dispatching marketing_events for the affected subject + transitions them to status='suppressed_by_consent'. Cancels pending DispatchToProviderJobs. This is the enforcement path for the re-check-at-dispatch consent guarantee — captured events with valid consent that are still in-flight when the user revokes are suppressed BEFORE they hit the wire.
hooks.note: Fires MarketingDeliverySuppressedByConsent (revoked_between_capture_and_dispatch=true) for each in-flight delivery affected.

hooks.name: CascadeMarketingProviderDeactivationOnTenantSuspended
hooks.purpose: Marks every marketing_provider_config for the suspended tenant as is_active=false. Does NOT soft-delete — restoration on tenant reactivation is one flip away (via ReactivateProvidersOnTenantResumed which checks the audit trail for reason='tenant_suspended'). Fires MarketingProviderDeactivated (reason='tenant_suspended') per config.
hooks.note: The DispatchToProviderJob observer refuses to dispatch through an inactive config, so in-flight events don't leak out during the suspension window.

no_bootstrapper.reason: Marketing does not need a tenant-boot hydration path — every operation is on-demand load from the DB. Contrast with modules like `tenancy` (bootstraps request-context) or `settings` (bootstraps config cache) that need per-request tenant hydration.
